channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 209 replies — 836.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 209 • Updated: 2026-07-09 16:28 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62460901
>>62437938
I just realized Larry Cheng wasn't even there, he is on a fucking monitor kek

62461047
>>62460901
He still cared enough to tune into that niche live show that happened on some random night outside of business hours.

62461136
Jake2SNEED is hosting a space on Twitter at noon on July 10th.

62461170
>>62461136
holy fucking shit bathroom gods are FEASTING

62461460
Tell me about Larry Cheng, why does it pump and dump the $GABBBYQ?

62461467
>>62460453
I do it because I know we are one. We are alot alike you and I, I fight for GameStop bussy whilst you defend the gape of $GABBBYQ, our bitches pump a different premium however.

62461479
PP, THE URSUrPER! ATTEMPTS TO TAKE THE GABBBBYQ DD FOR HIMSELF!

62461484
We fellow $GABBBYQ holders are the guardians of reading court documents fellas, keep scouring the internet for clues fellow buttholes!

62461501
whats up with the gabby posting? Why don't they try and explain why Pulte frequented the show or why Larry Cheng went on AFTER shares were cancelled? Why do they only rely on satire and harassment campaigns?

Why did the CEO and cofounder of the second largest market maker seethe so bad at an obscure BBBYQ recovery theory podcast that he went and found the real name of the podcast host, then started following twitter pages disparaging the host? How can a decamillionaire lose his cool so bad about a BBBYQ recovery theory podcast that he was force retired and barred from NHL involvement because of his rabid seething on twitter (some of which was non finance related to be fair)?

62461506
>>62461501
They can't answer the Cusip Conundrum directly so they created an nonsensical rebuttal. Simple, really.

62461509
>>62461501
Kinda funny after the midnight meeting on the 18th Pulte cut all ties with PP after he noticed Gabby sporting a black eye at the holiday inn, and she has not been seen since. Pulte has been doing everything in his power to climb the ranks of goverment to take down PP, it's all coming together

62461513
>insider information, it was his child

62461520
>>62461501
A number of people engaged in financial crimes relating to the demise of BBBY.

Now you guys go digging through every little detail of the case like crazy conspiracy theorists and you're likely to accidentally come across EVIDENCE OF CRIMES you weren't even looking for. And the fact is you won't even understand what you've found because it doesn't line up with your fantasies. But the criminals you accidentally and unknowingly expose are going to be PISSED. And they're going to try to make you look crazy. Which is not hard to do because you guys ARE crazy.

but even crazy people can be a threat if they poke their collective nose into enough dark corners.

62461580
>>62461520
Do not stand on ceremony anon

>Key Plays
PP
Pulte
Al Capone
Blues Brothers
Larry Cheng
Ryan Cohen
Mohammad
Bibi
Eric Trump

All involved in the corruption and gangbang of our precious. We must avenger her.

62461608
>>62461580
I don't know the specifics I'm just commenting in general

the thing is if you look deeply enough into anyone's life you're going to find stuff they don't want known. Everyone has skeletons in their closet and they want the closet door to remain firmly locked.

this is the main threat of doxxing. Not that people will find out who you are, but that they'll dig into your deets and find stuff you wanted to keep secret. Probably every millionaire alive has stuff they don't want exposed. Everyone does.

62461974
>Pulte acknowledged Teddy
>Sultan acknowledged Teddy
>Ryan Cohen already made the Teddy books
Teddy deniers are so stupid because it will literally be Ryan's magnum opus. Not only the perfect culmination of his efforts, but a beautiful homage to his late father, Teddy himself. This is what they make movies about.

62462883
>>62462840
Good thing I didnt actually buy bbby warrants.

62463362
>>62462840

62463530
>Alex & Gabbby court filings specify reasoning
>Adultery
OH KEKY WUCKYYYYYY How will this affect my 30000 GABBBYQ postion?

62463747
Why would seethie weethies follow a bbbyq recovery theory community so closely that they are searching for divorce docs of a bbbyq podcast host?

62463811
>>62463747
We are GABBBYQ enjoyers much like you, we take an interest much like you in court documents. PP hosted Larry Cheng at the PP show at the Holiday Inn

62463817
>>62463747
They hate PP so much in particular because he created a platform to talk about DD and the broader thesis in general. All of these filings are supposed to be clandestine in the wording and intentions so they can not be parsed through by retail very easily. You suddenly have hundreds of retail investors showing up in the exact stock at the worst possible time. They're clearly not used to retail investors operating in individual stocks like this and they are absolutely melting down over it. They are 100% engaging in criminal activity and they aren't too happy about all the eyes watching BBBYQ.

62463857
>>62463817
>we haven't read the court filings
How quaint, you claim to support PP but do not support PP

62463958
>>62463857
The problem you are having is that you think the internet is just going to pick up and run without whatever you want it too. Aside from me making some tendies here, I actually don't care at all about any of the personalities who talk about this stock. I meme my support for them only in relation in that there are shills here who hate his guts. You think you struck gold here and are desperate to create some sort of viral moment. But you really can't escape the truth of the matter: All of the hype here revolves around the systemic naked short sellers. PP as a grifter is meaningless in the grand scheme of things and you can't meme him in to being the bad guy no matter how hard you try. You can try to foster as much infighting as you want but it doesn't seem to stick, does it?

62464047
>>62463958
I literally do not care about any of these people beyond thinking PP should be prosecuted for running a pump and dump crypto scam.
>Promised not to sell his 'art project'
>Deleted promises not to sell his 'art project'
>Multiple wallets sold more 'art project' than they purchased
>Claimed that hedgies were shorting his 'art project'
>Claimed that the price of his 'art project' was being manipulated by the same algo manipulating GME
>Claimed that his 'art project' was targeted because of "his" DD
>Put himself next to DFV and RC

62464052
>>62464047
Literally none of those things bother me in the slightest lol.

62464166
>>62463958
I am sorry, but that means nothing, Larry Cheng came inside the PP show at the holiday inn, shillies have no dispute for this

62464263
>>62464047
Who the fuck cares? The fact that all of this bothers you more then having to wait and d word niggers having to perform in both threads is incredibly telling. Do a flip nigger.

item.title: Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news? — 75 replies — 300.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news?

Open thread • Replies: 75 • Updated: 2026-07-09 16:25 UTC

OP

Seems like every BTC pump or dump is blamed on ETF flows or macro headlines.

>Is that just the reality now, or are people overlooking other factors?

Early replies (first 3)

62450753
>>62450728
Its 100% cz dependent, just tradingview has to write something to the price action.

62450760
>>62450728
Btc does this thing where it lulls the newfag into a false sense of superiority. Both in bull and bear markets. In a bull market, it tricks newfags into thinking it will go up forever and traps them in overleveraged longs and in bear markets, it tricks them into it never going back up and they either sell low, sell covered calls or lever up short and get milked. Just hold spot and don't pay attention for a decade.

62450902
>>62450753
cz?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62462595
>>62461782
In what sense? Economically, politically, or something else?

62462696
wait for 10k dump

62462709
>>62450728
>has the coin sabotaged by glowniggers into becoming a "store of value" instead of digital cash lost all popular support as well as reason to exist and is now entirely propped up by the very institutions that made sure it'll never be in competition with them become too reliant on said institutions?

What

62462717
>>62462593
I'm more interested in TBVs. That's where I think the bigger opportunity is.

62462723
>>62462709
Bitcoin has become more than just a payments network. That's not the same as losing its reason to exist.

62462739
>>62462709
If Bitcoin has "no reason to exist," why are people still building new infrastructure around it?

62462938
>>62462717
Because it's not just the tech anymore. Aegis and Utila getting involved tells me there's real interest in native BTC.

62462943
>>62462739
>building new infrastructure around it?
I think that's called Ethereum

62462946
>>62462943
not bitcoin?

62462953
>>62462943
ethereum is the king of it

62462965
>>62462946
Nothing is being built around or on top of Bitcoin. When people hear tokenized assets or stablecoins are being built on crypto they think crypto = Bitcoin, but everyone who understands even a bit about crypto knows this is not true. If someone disagrees, feel free to shill whatever Bitcoin has achieved in the past 1 year, or in the past 5 years for that matter. I can't think of anything besides inscriptions, which had some potential but ended up as nothing more than speculative meme-NFTs. Bitcoin isn't designed to keep up with fast moving crypto tech, it's designed to be solid and ossified. It's biggest achievement is having not changed at all, monetarily or technologically.

62463137
>>62462965
You're only looking at the base layer. A lot of the innovation is happening around Bitcoin, not by changing Bitcoin itself.

62463151
>>62463137
Such as? Besides Lightning, which is mostly just custodial wallets, there is not a single Bitcoin L2 that's used.
>but bro they are in development muh 2 weeks away
This has been the case for 5+ years now.

62463201
>>62463151
Stacks, Lombard and BoB

62463256
>>62463151
>>62463201
Babylon is worth looking into if you think nothing's being built around bitcoin.

62463258
What's the best thing to do? Buy or sell

62463262
>>62450728

62463263
>>62463201
WBTC TVL is 10x more than all of these combined. There's way more BTC lending happening on Ethereum than on BTC L2s.

62463265
>>62450728
ETF’s = whales

62463279
>>62459225
You are forgetting every nations currency have the strength of wet noodles now. They will print again soon.

62463285
bitcoin is a commodity backed ETF without the commodity

all it ever is and all it ever will be

62463301
There should be a 90s themed live action series called something cheesy like "Good morning Mr. Nakamoto" about the bitcoin creator. After making it big in his mid 20s he has become a multi billionaire, but is extremely paranoid so he decided to keep a low profile. Now he's in his late 30s, a family man with a couple kids, and the series follow an episodic format where he desperately tries to hide from his family his enormous wealth and just pretends to be an office worker. The thing is he has zero credentials so he can't actually get a job so it becomes incrementally difficult (due to plot inducement stupidity) to maintain the farce. Prying yet endearing secondary characters keep coming and leaving, and oftentimes misunderstandings make him almost blow his cover. During some weaker seasons he's stalked by some group of nerds that are almost sure he's the real deal and want to take revenge due to prices plunging, but eventually some shenanigan makes the nerds pin their suspicions on Nakamoto's rival (who was trying to cuck him with his wife, who by the way goes through a very annoying arc because she realizes his hubby is not being open with her or something). Nakamoto's personality is a bit like Hal from Malcolm in the middle but less lolrandom, he just plays as a fool to better hide his secret.

62463320
>>62463301
Some thematic episodes could include his wife hiring a financial advisor because he played the broke ass bit too much, and he tries to stop the advisor from peeking at his 100b net worth at any cost. He also gets in trouble because the advisor is not very bright and tells terrible advice very overconfidently, so Nakamoto counters with suspiciously good and sharp analysis (fortunately his wife just doesnt care because he has ingrained the dumb with money narrative and the advisor is very confident, and also a financial advisor duh). In another episode he gets pinned by the IRS after accidentally doing some billion level transaction and having to pay tenths of millions in taxes. Another one where he (again, Plot) is revealed to have all the money behind a 20 word seed, but he doesnt remember the words, so it's a musical where he finally manages to recall them through a catchy song. The episode is rated quite poorly though because it doesn't fit with the general tone. In the seventh and last season his younger kid finds out by dumb luck and the rest of that season is him preventing them from telling mom. The show gets cancelled afterwards due do decline in viewership and the plot never gets finished.

62463429
>>62463301
>>62463320
This has potential

62464033
>>62463263
but it doesn't mean it'll stay that way.

62464098
>>62463429
I could foresee an actual ending where he has to let all his earnings go because of some contrived dumb reason, but it's framed in a way that looks somewhat deep and poignant. His cover is blown in the process, but as he's revealed to be the creator of Bitcoin, he suddently gets a call from one of the companies he tried to apply the most though all the seasons (let's say it's a mid-sized company that offers an average salary, it has it's wacky employees and boss, they usually appear for like 10 minutes every few episodes just to reject Nakamoto). He's finally accepted and he merrily joins (it's a subtle joke because now that he's a public figure he could easily be gunning for 500k jobs, but he settles for this as a nudge to the audience), managing to achieve the life he pretended to have (and actually wanted) from the get go. The series end with him walking into the office's slightly rundown and shabby building with a bright smile. You even get to make that cheap overused allegory of ''choosing happiness and being true to your principles over riches''. I feel like there's an alternate universe where this would have killed it.

62464115
>>62463256
I'll keep testing TBVs on Aave V4. BTC has a lot more going on now than just being a store of value.

62464121
>>62463301
The best ending would be nobody ever finding out if it's really him. Keeping the mystery alive is probably the most Satoshi thing possible.

62464142
>>62464098
You're too invested in that drama. It's not happening in real life.

62464251
>>62464121
You know what? That's fair. I relied too much on 90s sitcom tropes. It should just end episodically, there's just no more seasons and that's it.
>>62464142
I don't have much going on in my life and my family and friends find my occurrences boring. And it's supposed to be a comedy. What drama? I want it to be goofy and for all publics!

item.title: HOLY SHIT Reddit tells me there is an AI bubble that’s about to pop. — 41 replies — 164.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
HOLY SHIT Reddit tells me there is an AI bubble that’s about to pop.

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-07-09 16:36 UTC

OP

HOLY SHIT

Reddit tells me there is an AI bubble that’s about to pop. But what does /biz/ say?

Early replies (first 3)

62461969
>>62461964
Reddit is inherently Smarter than nu-4chan. It's why everyone on here sounds like a MW2 lobby, yelling and saying slurs. It's why 4chan leans right and reddit leans left

62461970
>>62461969
Retard

62461972
>>62461969
Shut up you retarded fucking faggot ass nigger

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62462469
>>62461964
All the news saying its a bubble
All the social media saying its a bubble.
All YouTubers saying it's a bubble.

Therefore it's not a bubble.

62462481
>>62462419
shes ugly though, so wouldnt

62462534
>>62461964
>muh capex muh revenue muh ratios muh supply and demand
>muh fundmantals
when will these retailoyim animals understand that the market is a schizophrenic female egregore? some fagot in a leather jacket can say something and telepathically inject billions in other companies

62462539
>>62461969
>left
>right
nobody who takes normalfag politics seriously is conscious or capable of rational discourse
>>62462419
>>62462356
every woman looks the same when you fuck them from behind
it makes their asses look massive and having a vagina massage your dick as you use it as a masturbation aid is an amazing feeling

62462564
>>62462469
The prophet Samuel Benner says it is a bubble. Therefore it is not a bubble!

62462565
AI will be deemed obsolete tomorrow and all the Tech companies will fire all the H1B visas working for 1/4 pay of their predecessors and bulldoze the datacenters and they will re-hire graphic artists to do human art by humans for humans...

62462591
They're right, nu-4chan is retarded. You're stupid if you buy AI stocks.

62463220
>>62462564
This chart has been wrong so many times yet normies believe it

62463229
>>62462481
I will do it and lick her feet.

62463231
If you are so worried then you should reallocate your positions away from the tech sector, there is no reason to be emotional when it comes to your own money.

62463232
>>62462469
I don't understand why this keeps getting parroted (Low IQ?).
People were screaming about bubble popping before 2000 and 2008 as well.
Just because it's mentioned doesn't nullify it from happening.

62463266
>>62463231
the majority of the S&P 500's gains over the last several years (really since COVID) are attributed to "big tech." also consider that if the bubble does pop, it's likely going to fuck up the broader economy and the labor market. there's no easy way to insulate yourself from AI niggermania.

62463268
AI isnt gonna go away

Is demand going to increase from here as they predict and their capex justifies? Probably not

62463455
>>62463232
Look at nasdaq in 2000 bubble on log scale. Realise how low iq you are.

62463482
>>62463266
But you would protect yourself from the direct collapse of AI centric companies. Nothing wrong with distributing into bonds/commodities for a period if you're anticipating a multi-year bear market. I do admit that there would still be a drawdown across most, if not all portfolios, but I'd rather shoulder a minor-moderate loss over gritting my teeth through some -50% scenario.

62463563
>>62461964
Apart from #4, then the article he is referring to is mostly right.
Most of these companies are incredibly intertwined, so if one of them goes down it will drag the rest down with them.
The business-model for AI hasn't really been fleshed out yet, there is a real risk that a lot of this Capex goes to expand into a low-markup market, or worse a non-existing market.

The strange thing is that AI is real. Artificial intelligence is real. Scientist, Doctors, Engineers live in the servers. A Butler, Nanny, Maid will live in your robot. A Driver in your car. An Accountant in your laptop.. etc.
The scale of AI is undeniable, the most transformative event in human history is now.

62463577
>>62462469
I read somewhere that "Black Monday" where in large part caused by people anticipating a crash.. Leading up to the crash, you had large number of bearish articles posted in the major news papers (back then everyone read the same few newspapers, so the impact of these were massive). So the mood really soured, which turned into selling, which turned into panic selling.

62463647
>>62463563
>the most transformative event in human history is now.
My little bean, they said this about railroads, electricity, radio, television, the internet, smartphones...

62463671
>>62463647
..and all of them probably were, at the time, the most transformative technology in human history.

62463680
>>62461964
I'm going to pop your ass.

62463695
>>62461964
1) What is investing in future revenue?
Do you think you earn as much as your CAPEX per year when building new business?
How do you measure risk and justifiable investment when trying to capture as much marketshare as possible in a new emerging market?
2) Same point as 1
3) Only shows the market is dominated by 5 big players
4) Workforce is typically the #1 cost for business and AI focuses on workforce optimisation. Artificial demand?
5) Plenty of these add their own value in one way or another.
Businesses that operate on AWS are vulnerable to price changes to virtual machines etc on AWS, same as the AI companies that utilize the tech of 3rd parties.

62463762
>>62461969
newfaggot
>reddit = retards pretending to be smart
>4chan = smart people pretending to be retarded

62463790
>>62463762

62463921
>>62463563
>t. AI salesman

62464146
>>62461964
Yes, but "about to" can mean in several more years. The eventual direction can be foreseen, but the exact timing and degree of the change is not so simple.

62464194
>>62463762
More like retarded people who think they’re smart pretending to be retarded

62464197
Everyone knows it's a bubble. But the funnel keeps bringing in more money. It's just a game of hot potato

62464272
>>62464197
everyone wants it to be a bubble because AI bad or something, it's more wishful thinking than anything

62464275
>>62461972
>>62461970
>>62463762
>Low IQ responses
On point

62464284
>>62463762
This was true maybe 15 years ago.

>>62464194
This is true now. This place is nothing but dunning-kruger late adopters now.

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 121 replies — 114.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 121 • Updated: 2026-07-09 15:34 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62442668
>>62442658
oy vey

62443383
>>62442650
Well, that's good for you.
>>62442655
Same. Self-custody first, then I check if the protocol has had multiple audits.

62443384
>>62442658
Do it man!

62443930
>>62442668
Goy

62444179
I'm waiting for October, but if it goes below 55k I'll probably start slurping. Everyone who says crypto I'd dead is retarded. Not ripping = dead is their logic.

62444587
>>62426537
That's never going to happen, max will be around 45K. My plan is to DCA, I got in around 58K, stake a few on babylon, I'm waiting to see if there will be more dump

62444608
>>62444587
You really trust the Babylon protocol? It's my first time looking into it and I see I could earn 0.02+ BTC every month. $1200+ every month sounds much better than sitting in my Ledger.

62444998
>>62444608
really

62445005
>>62426512
I'm selling at 100k. I've had enough of holding and should have sold last year. I wanna get out of this shit, it's too much stress.

62445018
>>62427357
do not pull

62445689
>>62444998
DYOR

62446816
>>62445018
Who's plebs rn?

62447133
>>62445005
regrets?

62447140
Ngmi

62447522
>>62442255
Just keep going

62448616
>>62426537
this is probably correct : (

trump and the political class already made their money off crypto last couple years

62448797
>>62427359
Surely the bottom would be zero, no?

62449904
>>62448797
people like to imagine when theyre on drugs

62450375
Why

62450895
Buy Bitcoin

62450912
>>62426512
Buy mote bitcoin and wait for next halving to dump on the normies.

62453141
>>62444608
The idea is to make BTC work even while holding just like ETH instead of leaving it idle, and I like the idea of self custody on that protocol

62454511
>>62426638
this but unironically

62454976
I sold at 115k so no more plans for me.

62455432
>>62454976
Ok

62455827
>>62426535
How you doing now, bobo?

62459212
>>62455827
Lmao

62459912
>>62444587
I like the idea behind their TBV, as it helps to keep all transactions on the BTC chain

62463121
>>62450912
Depends on you

62464112
>>62459912

item.title: /biz/ 8 years ago >BRO BUY THE DIP ON THIS SPECULATIVE COIN, IT'S GOING 100X! — 21 replies — 77.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
/biz/ 8 years ago >BRO BUY THE DIP ON THIS SPECULATIVE COIN, IT'S GOING 100X!

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-07-09 16:21 UTC

OP

/biz/ 8 years ago
>BRO BUY THE DIP ON THIS SPECULATIVE COIN, IT'S GOING 100X! YOU JUST NEED 1K DOLLARS
/biz/ 5 years ago
>OMG BRO SOMEONE THREW 3500 BUCKS/10 ETH INTO THIS COIN, WE HAVE WHALES! I'M UP 400K TODAY!
/biz/ now
>You should NEVER do calls, options, invest in any kind of crypto no matter the bull case, and if a stock is going up a lot you should be afraid because it will crash no matter what. Instead you should start a business- a real one, like contracting or landscaping, not an ecommerce business- and put your spare paychecks into VOO for 40 years.

Where did these people come from? The board is fucking dead but did we get an influx of unironic retired boomers giving out advice that 'worked' in 1966 and is now totally fucking worthless?

Early replies (first 3)

62453596
>>62453585
we've always had 3 or 4 boomers here. But now everyone else left.

coincidentally all those other "people" left when they made the capcha harder and made ban evasion more difficult.

62453601
>>62453585
Jannies killed the board. You cant share alpha or gambles or anything here now.

62453607
cant share any cryptos that arent in the top 100 coins lmao. the 2024/2025 janny (if theyre still here) turned this place into bag holder general

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62453619
>You should NEVER do calls, options

True, why do broke retards think they can beat rich smart people

62453628
The start a business fags are just trolling usually from what I see, provide no legit advice.
Invest in VOO fags should be banned just for being so boring. Why even bother to post this. Yes everyone knows put all your money in sp500 for 50 years you will make a few millions. Discussion is for how to beat this.

62453703
>>62453585
Its because making it in crypto is over, the newfag business retards and stock retards took over.
>>62453628
This, people here are retarded and think buying a broad stock index fund is some amazing genius plan but its the bare minimum to seperate yourself from a NPC.

62453842
Everything you posted was nonsense except the part about VOO

62453878
>>62453585
Circumstances have changed, I’m unironically smiling at my VOO and AIQ gains yet back then I was buying options and cryptos. All that money is gone now. I’m 30 now I need to think about my future seriously.
>>62453842
Based. We are expecting the 50% pullback and I will hold it like Charlie Munger says you ought to.

62453881
>>62453585
Its a glow psyop now.

62454979
>>62453585
maybe people learned not to waste money snd time on dangerous "investments"

62455028
>>62453878
>Based. We are expecting the 50% pullback and I will hold it like Charlie Munger says you ought to.
Like a billionaire who insider traded the market and lived in luxury until he died at almost 100 told you to?

62455042
>>62453601
>>62453607
This.
Try posting something that isnt a normie popular investment and it gets nuked instantly.

62455297
Nice thumbnail bro.

62455311
>>62453585
/biz/ now is more
>Start a power washing business and buy tech stocks

62460876
>>62460845
That's another gay bagholder meme that gets spammed on this board.

62461091
>>62460876
ur a gay bagholder meme

62461128
>>62453585
i just want to say i hate kikes, niggers, chinks, trannies, jeets and women
that is all

62461155
>>62461128
Why is bobo drinking piss?

62462421
>>62453585
>>You should NEVER do calls, options, invest in any kind of crypto no matter the bull case, and if a stock is going up a lot you should be afraid because it will crash no matter what. Instead you should start a business- a real one, like contracting or landscaping, not an ecommerce business- and put your spare paychecks into VOO for 40 years.

>Where did these people come from?

/biz/ had these guys in 2016 too lol, we've gone full circle

62464241
>>62453585

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2 — 58 replies — 75.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2

Open thread • Replies: 58 • Updated: 2026-07-09 15:51 UTC

OP

Brothers,

We spent 20 days and 203 posts together in the last thread and we have yet to formulate a method that will enable us to sit at the table in the six figure club with the rest of kinsmen. We knew this wouldn’t be easy so let's not get dispassionate.

For my next proposal I will say we focus on developing a skill that will increase our value. If we all level up on our own it will only take longer. If we all level up by 2-3 points a month then cumulatively we will all have gotten stronger as a mass society. Perhaps one amongst us can elevate themselves and come back to teach the others!

How long shall we be waiting then? How long will our own potential be mocked by others? To what limit will we continue to persist in the underclass?

Come, those who have not $10,000 to their name

Let us commence the general!

>Good /biz/ manga
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Good /biz/ movies
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Born_Rich_(2003_film)

>Good /biz/ books
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Gambler_(novel)

>Good /biz/ games
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Good /biz/ waifus
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elizabeth_Holmes

>Agenda 2030 is on the way, have you studied what your enemy has planned out?
https://www.math.uwaterloo.ca/~ervrscay/TheGreatReset.pdf

Praeviously
>>62373752

Early replies (first 3)

62453729
All you faggots need to do is buy chainlink. Fuck your side hustles. You can get a suicide stack for 8k right now.

62453795
Last thread we spoke a lot on learning new skills but very few fruitful things arose. I realized the issue is that a lot of the proposed skills were just boring or too niche.

62453799
>>62453795
wait, are you asking poor people to discuss amongst themselves how to get rich?
kek

you don't learn how to get rich from poor people

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62457877
>>62457799
How about this image?

What made this so special in 2021? A 6 billion market cap.

Artists made these, what did they know?

62457898
Well?

62457919
>>62457792
doge coin. started as a parody, forked from an existing blockchain. in the early days it was mine-able with a moderate GPU/CPU. i collected many thousands, forged from my own hardware. sadly, i succumbed to the notion it was only a parody and without value. the hard drive was formatted and those thousands of DOGE were not usable. i still regret the decision, however, i am happy to have had the experience.

>>62457669
>We shall learn about art!
afaik, all of the six figure earners dabble in the art world. it can be very profitable. there was a service being shilled by youtubers, as a way to make investments into fine art. can't remember the name of it.

62458178
>>62457919
No anon, Doge is an entirely separate Coin. This creature has a hat. Unless you mean to say Dogecoin attributed to it's success then I am in agreement.

Thank you for mentioning it though, that inherent quality that doge has so do it's derivatives.

I have to get bed as I have work tomorrow but I will continue this discussion tomorrow

62458289
My paycheck for one 13 hour shift of work + charges was 7.5k, like 5k after taxes.

62458294
>>62453622
Stupid general. Just live with your family or roommates and sell your car.
>N-No! You can't sell your car! How do I-
You will live. Actually you will thrive without it once you finally cut lose decades of car brained propaganda specifically made to put you in debt. In fact, you'll find that you can actually casually spend a lot more in eating out and subscriptions when you're not spending money on repairs, insurance and loans and so on for a deprecating asset that boomers keep trying to pressure you into buying at 16. You know I'm right too and even if you weren't sure I'm right, it's literally zero risk to find out because you can just buy a car again if it doesn't work out with the money you saved from not having a car lol

62458411
what do you guys think about buying 10k worth of btc and 10k of solana in a few months and holding until the next halvening?

it won't make you rich but you could expect modest 3x-8 from this at least right? it would be nice to go from 20k to 150k

62458425
>>62458411
3 to 8x is pretty generous. they would just give away free money like that. if you buy in the right season.

62458473
>>62458425
i know the rise and fall is becoming less and less, but a lot of people are scared to invest in it is my thoughts and its always 'the last bull cycle with big gains"

62458727
>>62458289
doing WHAT?

62458801
>>62453805
That's only $10k...

62458804
>>62453846
Poor.

62459556
>>62453729
>You can get a suicide stack for 8k right now.
For 7k now.
:)

62461612
Bump

62461928
Off work, now to devote my energies to this general

62461982
>>62457877
>>62457792
What sells a good memecoin is not only the narrative but the art accompanied alongside it. In this case, we know that

>Animals

Are positively received.

I cannot think of any legendary memecoin runner(multi-billion) that had a human at its front and center. It's usually animals, and commonly known animals like pets or frogs.

I think for this thread as we do our research on art we can also practice drawing as a way to become more valuable and ultimately increase our net worth.

62462009
Who here could obtain this job? All of you could, it will just come down to whether the individual hiring believes you have the qualifications.

Let's take a further examination into a high paying role such as this. Let's see what sort of requirements are needed to make $50 an hour.

62462035
you ever had a job that scaled like this?

62462047
Is this beyond your capabilities?

62462063
Is all your money in dollars?

Are you not aware that art itself can also be a place to store value in?

With a resurgence in European culture spreading out through the world, with the increase of non white races, won't people desire a form of art that was once dominant in the world?

ESPECIALLY as we get more and more digital?

62462167
>>62462035

>Question
>what makes art sell, what similarities did the memecoins ( >>62457792 ) &( >>62457877 )

Answer:
they were dogs

>Question
>what do we have to learn, make a high amount of money in a short amount of time?

Answer is right here --> >>62462047

Question
>What forms of art can we store money in

????

62462792
Have to go to bed,

Goodnight and bump

62462799
>>62457657
Must you broadcast your ethnic insecurity all over 4channel.org(slash) biz?

62462804
>>62454046
>mom look I posted it again!

62462813
I finally paid off my high Apr CC debt so now I can stack paychecks for a while. I’ve been good about cutting down spending in general. I wonder if I should cut out meat and just do rice and beans? Can you get similar nutrition from beans? Things are certainly not looking great because I care about my job less by the day but at least I paid off debt.

I don’t wanna /blog too hard but fuck life is sucking lately, I just hope it’s better in a year. I been working nonstop for two years haven’t had any pto either

62462824
>>62453622
>yet to formulate a method that will enable us to sit at the table in the six figure club with the rest of kinsmen
go work literally any job for like 6 months

62462992
>UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2

OH NO NO NO

AHAHAHAHAHAHA

LMAOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

62464145
>>62462813
Rent truly is the worst bloodsucker. What are you saving for? Just to have reserve funds? You were spending all your excess money on debt and not saving/investing? Genuinely asking

62464163
>>62462813
Also I would be careful with poor nutrition depending on your age. If under 25 then take the risk with cheap foods.

item.content:encoded:
16,000 LINK and Counting

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-07-09 16:20 UTC

OP

Niggers, jannies, and fudders TONGUE MY ANUS. Also, is it over for BAT? I still have a pile of it and it's bleeding out faster than Charlie Kirk

Early replies (first 3)

62461808
p+c, stinkie
pee plus heckin' see

62461810
>>62461808
Kek. You again? Bend over and lick my taint, tranny

62461938
>>62461796
damn, you're gonna have 16 million dollars soon. are you at least staking your own 10k? and yeah its absolutely over for BAT, i still have 20k and i will always be pissed it never mooned. i saw a brave ad recently showing people tipping 200-500 BAT, its clear they intend it to stay at .20 cents forever.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62461946
>>62461938
I can't get in the fucking pool since I'm a latefag. But, I don't really care. I'll keep it until if and when they expand it. But yeah. I saw that ad too. Fucking disaster. Roadmap V3 was just a series of bullshit partnerships and a memecoin .Roadmap V4 will make that look good by comparison. Fucking rent seekers

62461966
>>62461946
its been a disaster since 2020 when the entire world needed a safe way to have video calls and brave had it built in, they just never pushed it or advertised it. so many great features of brave go unused because they stopped developing them or they don't tell anyone about them. i'm not even signed up for BAT rewards because i don't want to sign up for uphold. its a big disappointment all around. if i had chosen LINK over BAT from the beginning i'd already be a millionaire. now im only at 4064 link.

62461987
>>62461966
Checked. The sad part is I believe Luke means well. The rest of the team, Jennie and Lobes included, just don't give a fuck. Lobes is holding out for a merger of some sort for sure so that he can have his last boomer pay day. I keep holding hoping for a pump to $1 so I can get a decent 7x on my investment but even that seems impossible now.

62462468
>>62461987
i wouldn't worry too much if i were you, you're about to have 16 million dollars. after taxes probably something like 12 million dollars. you're set for life.

62462785
Uh... thots?

62464025
>>62462468
One can hope.
>>62462785
TP abandoned us bro

62464235
>>62461796
based link holder.

BEARISH ON COSTCO

Open thread • Replies: 128 • Updated: 2026-07-09 15:41 UTC

OP

People are scamming costcos lenient return policy now. Should we short COST?

Video:
https://x.com/Basedgoymemes/status/2073860759269077199

Early replies (first 3)

62449396
based

62449400
I wouldn't call jeets 'people'

62449441
Fuck this racist, post the person who started to do this was some white bitch, she did it for 12 years

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62456250
>>62456057
Kek

62456293
>>62455930
Kek, we stopped letting you dirty fuckers in last year, we have enough people serving coffee and doing menial jobs already.
You faggots are all 5'6" and stink like either vile BO or a whole bottle of nigger-scented baby powder wash.
Being brown and ugly is a curse from god himself; you are marked brown like shit and can never wash it off. Ugly jeet kek

62456323
>>62449769
Your skin colour is the same as my poop
>>62451853
>jelly
The only "jelly" thing is your jamboi covered body

62456610
>>62449441
Yes this saar is correct do not be insulting India sats, India is in the bag!!

62457469
IZZATED

62457562
>>62457469
Feels good to be a winner, conquering new lands, getting rich. America is so welcoming!

62457973
>>62457562
Cope

62458144
>>62454135
They've conquered my city, not my mind. They are rude, inconsiderable, foul-smelling pricks and are insufferable to be around. They practically camp out at CostCo now. I'd rather shop at smaller, more expensive stores just to avoid their presence. There is nothing I can do about it.

62458606
>>62458144
Canadian ?

62458608
>>62458144
There is plenty you could do but you value your comfort over your country

62458622
>>62449846
LMAO

62458858
The jugaad is crazy

62460566
Jugaadjeet

62460624
>>62449441
Bloody bitch bastard

62460700
>>62449388
this is old news
I do this.
>18 months later mattress topper starts to hurt my back
>Whats the reason for the return?
>it hurts my back
>ok thank you sir, here is full refund

or the guy who returned his inflatable pool after a few months. He got the pool for his pet penguin that he ordered. after a few months and no penguin delivery he realized it was a scam and returned the pool and other things he got for the penguin.
>reason for return
>penguin order was a scam, no penguin arrived

I also know some dudes who buy wavestorms, go fuk em up, and exchange them

Costco puts the onus of returns on the manufacturer or supplier as a price for getting on the shelf in the store. just having a spot in costco is worth it enough to most companies to front all the returns for costco's lax return policy. Which is why costco DGAF and rather have customers.

62460720
>>62458144
serious question
I went bowling last week. There was a huge group of brown men. Enjoying themselves, very much, maybe too much.
They can not keep their hands off each other it was the strangest thing I've every seen.
Rubbing, patting, petting....
Each time 1 went to bowl they had to break free of all these hands and arms.
It was like a race to be the last one to touch the bowler, right up until they've thrown. like the hands and arms in drag me to hell expect brown and sensual
so bizarre.
why do they do this?
pic rel but brown and stinky

62460768
>>62455914
I for one love my country. And like true love I hate all those that threaten to jeopardize it. Enjoy your stay but you have to go back

62460799
fun fact: indians skin is brown because they each eat up to 4lbs of poop daily, like how flamingos turn pink from their diet of shrimp!

62460937
>>62460799
Oh I thought flamingos turned pink because they ate pussy

62461696
saaaaaaaaaaaars

62462254
>>62460799
wtf

62462262
>>62449388
All these office chairs are basically painted fabric. The paint flakes off after a while. They shouldn't be able to sell this junk in the first place.

62462271
>>62449388
>People
americans are not people.

62462287
>>62449441
Why do Indians talk so weird? It's like you can immediately tell they're jeets just from text alone

62462621
>>62462271

62462950
>>62449388

62462960
>>62453201
i would cover her face with semen if you catch my drift

62463582
>>62449441
BENCHOOOOOD!!!!!!! WHY YOU REDEEM!! NO NO DO NOT CLICK ON THAT!!!! NOOOOOOOOOOO!!!!!!

62463635
>>62458606
cucks here won't do shit, they bitch and complain while patronizing Indian establishments. They will talk about the take over and then also about their favorite totally awesome pajeet they met at signhortons. The most opposition you will get is from white canadians. People will whine about the state of tim hortons while simultaneously pretending a coffee from there is a mandatory purchase they will die without

Sad, but they will have to watch canada just disintegrate with no option to fix it before they maybe realize. This whole country is full of just chicken shit low IQ cattle. Maybe 4% of canadians realize how bad this actually is and are doing the bare minimum like not hiring and giving money to the invaders, the rest are fully, entirely complicit in it. If it wasn't for how big this place was, that you can find thousands of acres with not a single person within 5km of you, I would have already left.

62464135
>>62463582
Saaars

item.title: Swift is rolling out its own blockchain right now in live production — 48 replies — 32.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Swift is rolling out its own blockchain right now in live production

Open thread • Replies: 48 • Updated: 2026-07-09 15:09 UTC

OP

DTCC is going live on-chain later this year.
Yet crypto is in the shitter, why?

Early replies (first 3)

62463146
>>62463144
Explain how it's bullish for crypto?

62463270
>>62463146
Both Swift and DTCC are going to be using a variety of public chains.

62463273
So SWIFT has their own singular chain. I thought it'd be that each bank has their own chain, connected to each other by CCIP. If it's all done on a single chain then it obviates the need for a lot of CCIP transactions, right?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62463686
>>62463144
>Swift and DTCC go live within the next weeks
what would theoretically happen if wake up and one linke equals suddenly $5000

62463688
>>62463670
Live = live.

Cope with it.

62463703
>>62463688
>how many fingers do i have up
>i... i don't know....
>correct, stinkie.

62463708
>>62463703
I said "live in production" in OP, and that's exactly what's happening.
Clearly you think "pilot" means something it actually does not.

62463727
>>62463708
>in nine months
>pilot live transactions
>could support future innovations
all from your own OP, but good attempt at gaslighting
it's literally nothing, see ya in 9 months

62463739
>>62463727
>>in nine months
They mean it was nine months ago that they announced this ledger, and now (nine months later) they're moving into production.

Funny that you keep switching between IDs too.

62463752
>>62463739
touche
>preparing to pilot
>preparing
>to pilot
>could support future innovations
>could support
>future innovations
this is quite literally the definition of announcements of announcements, your coping is hilarious
I haven't swapped a single ID itt, stop projecting your gaslighting upon others, paid shiller

62463767
>>62463752
>they didn't release at 100% full scale
>therefore it's not happening
top jej

The strange thing is that you're so passionate about this too.
Clearly you were hoping Swift was gonna use YOUR chain instead of building their own.

62463796
>>62463767
they never will anon, by all means keep waiting tho
>YOUR chain
what are you talking about bro? are you schizophrenic? who are you talking to? what is MY chain?

62463808
>>62463796
which one are you bro

62463819
>>62463808
none of them bro, what now? I don't even have a xitter account fyi
btw you're mentally ill in case you didn't know
so let me bite, WHEN does this usage from 17 banks begin? cause your OP is just stating that they're "ready"
if you can't tell me WHEN they will actually start testing this, then this is quite literally an announcement of an announcement

62463835
>>62463819
"Announcement of an announcement" goes like this "stay tuned for a big announcement".
This is simply the announcement: "we're going live".

>you're mentally ill
says the faggot posting on a crypto board pretending he doesn't hold any crypto

62463837
>>62463835
>This is simply the announcement: "we're going live".
when?
>posting on a crypto board pretending he doesn't hold any crypto
what the fuck are you talking about bro?

62463883
>>62463837
>when?
What do you even mean kek
Are you saying Swift and those 17 initial banks are lying?

>what the fuck are you talking about bro?
The fact that you're pretending not to have "MY chain".

62463901
>>62463883
Seriously, lurk more you stupid faggot. I don’t want to see another post of yours for at least 6 months

62463904
>>62463883
I'm asking when are they going live bro
>you're pretending not to have "MY chain"
what in the flying fuck are you talking about dude? you're completely deranged

62463912
>>62463883
lurk more, retard

62463914
>>62463904
>I'm asking when are they going live bro
Why are you asking me?
If Swift and those 17 banks are lying, that's on them and not me.

>what in the flying fuck are you talking about dude?
I asked you what "your chain" was, and you implied you didn't have any.

62463917
>>62463901
>>62463912
what are you even sperging about

62463923
>>62463917
just lurk.
stop posting. and lurk.

62463939
>>62463923
>get demolished
>switch IDs and start randomly sperging in non-sequiturs

classic

62463946
legit mental illness, and all this because I dared to question his shilling by simply asking WHEN are those banks going live
dear God
>my ID
>muh YOUR CHAIN
go back to the asylum you unhinged schizoid

62463963
>>62463946
Why are you asking anons for the exact time each of those 17 banks makes their first transaction on this new Swift ledger?
Clearly this is upsetting you greatly.

62463968
Whats up biepum?
Hows that flippening coming along?
Boxing with shadows again I see?
Touch grass baggie

62463973
>>62463968
ID-that-spews-random-butthurt-non-sequiturs number 4

62463981
>>62463939
>multiple people want me to stop posting because all i do is fall for scams and generally make the board a worse place
>tHeY'rE sWiTcHiNg IdS

62463996
>>62463981
>multiple people

62464019
don't bother, the guy is completely unhinged, he's spamming multiple threads doing the same shit, look at this>>62464006

62464021
>>62464019
doing what shit?

62464030
chainlink not used

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 132 replies — 21.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 132 • Updated: 2026-07-09 10:20 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62457986
>>62453537
Is the card market too inflated/saturated to bother anymore? Or do you still scalp?

62458076
>>62457793
>Honesty is important and if I catch a whiff of a liar in an interview its over, but in your case this anon is right. You have to lie on your resume, a 20 year gap would not make it past HR to me ever. You must research enough to be able to answer questions about what you put on there as work history so don't go too crazy. Such as explain to me in detail exactly what you did day to day at your previous made up job.

This is a low key admission job history doesnt matter.

62458190
>>62457884
No that doesn’t exist here. You can’t get a government job unless you’re a gay disabled Jewish black female veteran.

62458213
>>62449961
Do a trade or some type of apprenticeship through military or come to Australia and do FIFO (fly in fly out work). High pay, stack LINK. There's always hope fren

62458240
>>62450003
Depends on your country, but it's a good place to save by milking the free stuff.
Pick a civie equivalent job that isn't too physical and coast.

If you're not stupid enough to buy expensive cars and burn money on strippers you can save a lot (hookers are a more efficient use of spending money on women).
Get in, get out and collect gibs if you Jew your medical correctly

62458604
>>62449961
why do retards who ask how to get jobs never, EVER talk about their skills, experience (yes, you can be a NEET and still have experience even if you have never had a job), hobbies (that can be converted to jobs) and interests?
not asking about this particular OP, but about people in general...

62458671
You've got me searching for stuff from high school now. 20 years ago.
Jesus.

62458676
>>62458604
Because they don't think they have any skills or experience. And how would they evaluate that anyway? They have no frame of reference.

62458726
>>62458676
being ignorant about their own employable skills is exactly the reason why they should mention skills, experience, hobbies and interests.
they could simply say "I like X, Y and Z. do you think any of these things can help me get a job?". even gaymers can work as testers.


I was a NEET once upon a time, and I knew I liked computers, reading science and engineering news and stuff like that. that got me a job.

62458735
just end it bro

62458848
>>62449961
kind of fucked
your best bet if you have a car is to get a job working overnight at McDonalds or another 24 hour fast food place
if you aren't obviously a druggy or look like a psycho they will hire you on the spot, seriously
or just lie on your resume and get any menial wageslave job
just research some businesses that used to exist in your area, for example find restaurants that went out of business and say you worked as a dishwasher
if they ask about gaps in your resume you can say you were looking after a sick family member
make everything before the last 5 years or so a small business that you owned and operated yourself, for example lawnmowing

so your resume should look like this
graduated high school
worked for yourself as a believable small business like a lawnmower
"my business was destroyed by covid"
began looking after a sick relative for 2-3 years
worked as a dishwasher, or cleaning toilets, or some other thing for a business like a hotel that went out of business recently in your town or a nearby town. for 2 or so years until 6-12 months ago when that business closed
been searching for a job since then

problems with this method:
- if they check references you're fucked but no one does for these dead end jobs like McDonalds maintainence or dishwasher. there are services you can hire which pretend to be references for you but it shouldn't be necessary
- if you don't have a car you're just fucked, you need it to have a leg up on other applicants for these jobs. I'm pretty sure I was hired by McDonalds the moment I said I had my own vehicle and could be available to start at 2am
- if you get the job you're trapped in a hell dead end wage slave job

source: same situation as you OP but I was 29 rather than 39

62459478
>>62458735
Why, my life is better than yours.
>>62458848
I live miles from any McDonald's. I've got a convenience store, a place that used to be a bowling alley but turned into some other business I've never been in, OR a several mile trip along a freeway.

62460298
>>62449961
>>62449979
Some sort of low entry Zogbot job;
Coast Guard is easy
National Guard idk
Navy
Airforce

Avoid anything army related and you'll be alright.

t. Same boat but turned it around

62460390
op unironically save up enough money for a pressure washer and sign up for one of those indepdent contractor apps and start pressure washing boomers drive ways. save up enough to buy or lease a car then pivot into driving uber.


this is your only realistic path to making any kind of money beyond minimum wage

62460633
>>62460390
By driving for Uber? You okay man? You wanna talk about it?

62460654
>>62449961
Most strip malls have a staffing agency that would jump if anyone without a felony came in. It'll suck at the beginning but that's what you get for wasting 23 years of your life.

62460716
>>62460633
?

uber drivers crack $40/hour where I am

62460737
>>62460716
Well, that's unusual. They're getting very lucky.

62460759
>>62460737
I’d be surprised if they don’t make at least $25 an hour in most metro areas and the peripheries

where do you live if you don’t mind my asking that they are legitimately only making minimum wage?

62460789
>>62460759
>make $25 an hour
>spend double that on gas and vehicle maintenance.
How exactly is that supposed to pan out?

62460802
>>62460789
maintenance isn’t that much if you have a newer car, and I’m pretty sure driving some shit box is against uber rules anyway you need to register with a relatively new car


gas eh maybe you have a point but they’re still out so it’s obviously profitable

I highly doubt uber drivers are making minimum wage except in some super rural area where everyone had a car. And I know for a fact they make decent money where I live

62461444
>>62460802
So I need a high paying job to get a nice new car so I can ruin it working for a lower paying job?
I live in California, gas alone would fuck me sideways. If I had a nice car I'd leave. Go to Oregon or Washington.

62461781
I just realized I'm going to need to learn how to pay taxes.

62462300
>>62461781
https://www.irs.gov/individuals/free-tax-return-preparation-for-qualifying-taxpayers if you make less than 69k a year you probably qualify

62462365
trucking. self-driving trucks are a meme and still decades away, if you're a company driver you can pull down 80k clean with little overhead.

most companies will hire you on the spot if you have a CDL and a clean record

62462409
>>62459478
yeah you arre going to have to drive miles to your job
you DO have a car right op?

62462413
>>62462365
>if you have a CDL
they have pivoted to only hiring jeets as of a year or two ago

62462416
>>62460802
it's all jeets and other browns that cheat the system by having 3 drivers share the same car and uber account
you're lucky to make minimum wage after gas, tax and wear and tear

62462448
>>62462416
Actually, sharing a car makes sense. You at least split the cost of buying a car between you. And the insurance.

62463196
>>62462448
Triple the maintenance, but you have support from your other drivers

item.title: VOO and Chill — 203 replies — 14.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 203 • Updated: 2026-07-09 02:44 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62436507
>>62400636
>Yes and I'm sure that used car and maybe a holiday you can buy with it when you're 65 from cashing out all your investments will be great.....
You say this as if the average /biz/ shitcoin gambler is performing better than VOO which is definitely not the case.

62436510
>>62428031
Your IQ is so actually low. Holy shit.

62436538
>>62436510
Do you think DRAM has value, honest question, and for how long - if so, what dip should one wait for
>>62436499
VGT and QQQ are only doing better thanks to tech having such strong performances for years now, correct?
VOO seems safe since you don't know the future, but on the other hand, thinking the future of the market for the next decades wont always be tech focused seems so short sighted it's silly.....maybe I should start DCA into VGT?

62436562
>>62400493
>VOO
it's EVOO for me lads

62438775
Yep bros
Bought some more VOO and am waiting on my DRAM position to go green so I can sell and not have to deal with thematic ETFs no high expense ratios ever again
Simple as

62440169
>>62431374
>VOO is managed by 2 girl bosses
VOO is passively managed. These 2 girls are not like Kathy Wood picking stocks.

62442752
>>62431374
They could boil alive eat grannies alive for all I care as long as they get the money going, what kind of faggot ass train of thought is this. They could become billionaires and I would not give a shit as long as it keeps compounding. "The people making me rich are foids so it makes me icky" is unironically the foidest take I have ever read.
>>62430902
A year ago I started investing for the first time in my life (I'm in my mid 20s) and thought I was very smart and I would just outperform the market 1% profit at a time. After a whole year I only managed to get a net benefit of a 3% after wasting many hours and carrying heavy bags. I learnt my lesson. VOO and chill.

62442988
>>62442752
>They could become billionaires and I would not give a shit as long as it keeps compounding.

The portfolio managers of VOO have 0 control over whether VOO compounds or not. VOO is simply an ETF that tracks the S&P 500. Their only job is to make sure VOO follows the S&P 500. Once the fund is set up, there really is not much for them to do. I guess if they haven't automated removing and adding companies to the composition of VOO, then they do that.

62444521
Let’s all say thank you to Jack Bogle

62444531
>>62400515
and yet boomers hold 90% of the wealth and growing...

62445651
>>62421799
>5+ yr 60% drawdown event happens
>buy cheaper voo

62446571
>>62444521
thanks chad bogle

62447653
>>62445651
Kek it really is that simple

62450991
>>62446571
Thanks Chad Bogle!

62454214
>>62413718
Felix Prehn has never been an investment banker. He is an ex artist and wine salesman, now grifter on YouTube who makes money from his "academy" fees (up to 10k for shitty training). He's a regular scammer, like many in this segment.

62455460
It feels good to be a VOO’er

62455475
>>62400493
My retirement funds are $70k and in VOO, anything better to put all that in?

62455990
>>62455475
Look into VPMAX

62456048
>>62455990
Neat

62459187
>>62403053
The median 64 yo is planning to work until they're dead.
Boomers are never going to retire because they can't afford to. They are going to keep working like the good little goyim they are and fall down dead on the job because that's the only way they can fund the monthly payments on all the toys they went into debt to purchase.
I spit on their graves. Fuck boomer scum.

62459238
>>62459187
Lmao, another white incel who knows everything,,,,,what are the odds? 100% at least,,,,,do they use it to get rich? Lmao no

62460695
Yawwwwwn another week another VOO purchase

62460733
>>62460695
i made a whole $5 buying a share of VOO during that dip :) too bad I lost more from all my previous VOO shares

62460835
>>62460733
Sorry to hear that champ maybe DRAM is more your speed

62460844
>>62400995
Lmfao. You do realize that the highest weighted stocks that lead the spy are mostly in QQQ right? Lmfao

62460852
>>62459187
They can afford it

62461238
>>62401051
>I think a decade long CAGR comparison was a 5% difference but I’m not sure if CAGR is the best stat to go by?
It changes significantly depending on when you enter the market. Since 2000 there's only a 0.6% difference in favor of QQQ over SPY, the latter outperformed the former for most of that timeframe. If you bought sometime post-2009, however, you would have been better off holding QQQ by far. The moral of the story is keep an eye on things and only buy more volatile assets if it's a growth period. QQQ will continue to be great if you bought a decade ago until the music stops.

62461412
>>62400515
Fpbp. Bogleheads are the biggest retards on the planet.

62461672
>>62400620
>What makes one rich
Health, family, friends.

62462476
>>62461238
For now I’m still just doing VOO cause it just feels better even if I’m missing out on qqq gains
